info.name: Knock Users API
info.description:
Identify, list, update, delete, and merge users plus manage their channel data, preferences, in-app feeds, guides, subscriptions, messages, and schedules. The primary recipient surface for Knock.

Contact Support:
 Name: Knock

variable.description: (Required) The unique identifier of the user.

request.description: Records that a guide has been seen by a user, triggering any associated seen events.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: bearer

query.description: The tenant ID to use for targeting and rendering guides.
The data (JSON encoded object) to use for targeting and rendering guides.
The type of guides to filter by.

variable.description: (Required) The unique identifier of the user.

request.description: Returns the feed settings for a user.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: bearer

query.description: The status of the feed items.
The workflow key associated with the message in the feed.
The tenant associated with the feed items.
Whether the feed items have a tenant.
The workflow categories of the feed items.
The workflow categories of the feed items.
The archived status of the feed items.
The trigger data of the feed items (as a JSON string).

The locale to render the feed items in. Must be in the IETF 5646 format (e.g. `en-US`). When not provided, will default to the locale that the feed items were rendered in. Only available for enterprise plan customers using custom translations.
Comma-separated list of field paths to exclude from the response. Use dot notation for nested fields (e.g., `entries.archived_at`). Limited to 3 levels deep.

The mode to render the feed items in. Can be `compact` or `rich`. Defaults to `rich`. When `mode` is `compact`, feed items will not have `activities` and `total_activities` fields; the `data` field will not include nested arrays and objects; and the `actors` field will only have up to one actor.
The cursor to fetch entries after.
The cursor to fetch entries before.
The number of items per page (defaults to 50).
Limits the results to items inserted after the given date.
Limits the results to items inserted after or on the given date.
Limits the results to items inserted before the given date.
Limits the results to items inserted before or on the given date.

variable.description: (Required) The unique identifier of the user.
(Required) The unique identifier for the channel.

request.description:
Returns a paginated list of feed items for a user in reverse chronological order, including metadata about the feed. If the user has not yet been identified within Knock, an empty feed will be returned.

You can customize the response using [response filters](/integrations/in-app/knock#customizing-api-response-content) to exclude or only include specific properties on your resources.

**Notes:**
* When making this call from a client-side environment, use your publishable key along with a user token.
* This endpoint’s rate limit is always scoped per-user and per-environment. This is true even for requests made without a signed user token.
* Any [attachments](/integrations/email/attachments) present in trigger data are automatically excluded from both the `data` and `activities` fields of `UserInAppFeedResponse`.

response.name: OK

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: bearer

query.description: The status of the feed items.
The workflow key associated with the message in the feed.
The tenant associated with the feed items.
Whether the feed items have a tenant.
The workflow categories of the feed items.
The archived status of the feed items.
The trigger data of the feed items (as a JSON string).

The locale to render the feed items in. Must be in the IETF 5646 format (e.g. `en-US`). When not provided, will default to the locale that the feed items were rendered in. Only available for enterprise plan customers using custom translations.
Comma-separated list of field paths to exclude from the response. Use dot notation for nested fields (e.g., `entries.archived_at`). Limited to 3 levels deep.

The mode to render the feed items in. Can be `compact` or `rich`. Defaults to `rich`. When `mode` is `compact`, feed items will not have `activities` and `total_activities` fields; the `data` field will not include nested arrays and objects; and the `actors` field will only have up to one actor.
The cursor to fetch entries after.
The cursor to fetch entries before.
The number of items per page (defaults to 50).
Limits the results to items inserted after the given date.
Limits the results to items inserted after or on the given date.
Limits the results to items inserted before the given date.
Limits the results to items inserted before or on the given date.

request.description: Permanently delete a user and all associated data.

variable.description: (Required) The ID of the user to retrieve.

request.description: Retrieve a specific user by their ID.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: bearer

variable.description: (Required) The unique identifier of the user.

request.description: Create or update a user with the provided identification data. When you identify an existing user, the system merges the properties you specific with what is currently set on the user, updating only the fields included in your requests.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: bearer

request.description: Bulk sets the preferences for up to 1,000 users at a time. The preference set `:id` can be either `default` or a `tenant.id`. Learn more about [per-tenant preferences](/preferences/tenant-preferences). Note that this is a destructive operation and will replace any existing users' preferences with the preferences sent.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: bearer

request.description: Identifies multiple users in a single operation. Allows creating or updating up to 1,000 users in a single batch with various properties, preferences, and channel data.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: bearer

request.description: Permanently deletes up to 1,000 users at a time.
